headline: SEC Delays Tokenized Stock Trading Proposal Amid Pushback; Crypto Exchanges Slide
summary: The Securities and Exchange Commission delayed a proposal that would allow crypto firms to trade tokenized stocks, reports say.

headline: CWB’s 19% YTD Return Shows How Convertible Bonds Bridge Stock Gains and Income Floor
summary: Convertible bonds sit in an odd corner of capital markets: corporate debt with an embedded equity option, paying coupon income while carrying upside tied to the issuer’s stock. The SPDR Bloomberg Convertible Securities ETF (NYSEARCA:CWB) is the largest fund built around that hybrid, with roughly $5 billion in assets and a 0.40% expense ratio. CWB ... CWB’s 19% YTD Return Shows How Convertible Bonds Bridge Stock Gains and Income Floor

headline: Coinbase and Other Crypto Stocks Are Falling. Several Possible Reasons for the Bad Day.
summary: What looked like it would be a decent end to the week for crypto stocks turned sour Friday.  The price of  Bitcoin  fell by 0.9% over the past 24 hours, and  Ethereum  slipped by 0.5%.  The stocks began Friday trading about flat before tumbling in a half-hour span beginning around 10 a.m. Eastern Time.

headline: Solmate Infrastructure CEO & Board Member Invest $11.4M in Company Shares
summary: By Karen Roman Brera Holdings PLC (Nasdaq: SLMT), also called Solmate, said CEO Ron Sade and Board Member Keren Maimon jointly purchased about 2.3 million Class B shares at a premium of $4.97 per share, for around $11.4 million. The transaction is expected to close on May 27, 2026, supporting management’s and the Board’s conviction […]

headline: Why crypto firms are trying to become Wall Street infrastructure companies
summary: The next phase of crypto’s public-market pitch is less about token prices and more about financial infrastructure. As trading revenue slows, companies including Coinbase, Robinhood, Circle and Bullish are trying to show they can own the systems that issue, trade, settle and record assets. The question for investors is whether these new businesses can grow fast enough before the next crypto downturn puts the model under pressure again.
